---
title: External Events
description: Coordinate with external systems using promises and signals in Workflows
---

Workflow coordination helpers are available only in Workflow functions for managing long-running processes, external events, and human interactions. These helpers enable sophisticated coordination patterns.

## ctx.promise()

Create promises that external systems can resolve to send data to your workflow. This is how workflows wait for external events, human input, or other services to provide data.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Wait for external payment confirmation
const paymentResult = await ctx.promise<PaymentResult>("payment-completed");

// Wait for human approval
const approved = await ctx.promise<boolean>("manager-approval");

// Wait for multiple events
const [payment, inventory] = await Promise.all([
  ctx.promise<PaymentResult>("payment-confirmed"),
  ctx.promise<InventoryResult>("inventory-reserved")
]);
```

```java Java
// Wait for external payment confirmation
PaymentResult paymentResult = ctx.promise("payment-completed", PaymentResult.class).await();

// Wait for human approval
Boolean approved = ctx.promise("manager-approval", Boolean.class).await();

// Wait for multiple events
CompletableFuture<PaymentResult> payment = ctx.promise("payment-confirmed", PaymentResult.class);
CompletableFuture<InventoryResult> inventory = ctx.promise("inventory-reserved", InventoryResult.class);
PaymentResult paymentResult = payment.await();
InventoryResult inventoryResult = inventory.await();
```

```python Python
# Wait for external payment confirmation
payment_result = await ctx.promise("payment-completed", PaymentResult)

# Wait for human approval
approved = await ctx.promise("manager-approval", bool)

# Wait for multiple events
payment_task = ctx.promise("payment-confirmed", PaymentResult)
inventory_task = ctx.promise("inventory-reserved", InventoryResult)
payment_result, inventory_result = await asyncio.gather(payment_task, inventory_task)
```
</CodeGroup>

### Promise keys

Choose descriptive promise keys that clearly indicate what event you're waiting for:

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Good promise keys
await ctx.promise<ApprovalResult>("manager-approval-12345");
await ctx.promise<PaymentResult>("payment-confirmation-order-456");
await ctx.promise<CallbackData>("webhook-callback-user-789");

// Less clear keys
await ctx.promise<any>("data");
await ctx.promise<any>("result");
await ctx.promise<any>("callback");
```
</CodeGroup>

## ctx.promise().resolve()

Resolve promises from signal functions to send data to waiting workflows.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Signal function to confirm payment
export const confirmPayment = async (ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, result: PaymentResult) => {
  ctx.promise("payment-completed").resolve(result);
};

// Signal function for human approval
export const approveRequest = async (ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, approved: boolean) => {
  ctx.promise("manager-approval").resolve(approved);
};

// Signal function with complex data
export const submitApplication = async (ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, application: ApplicationData) => {
  ctx.promise("application-submitted").resolve({
    applicationId: application.id,
    submittedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    data: application
  });
};
```

```java Java
// Signal function to confirm payment
@Handler
public void confirmPayment(WorkflowSharedContext ctx, PaymentResult result) {
    ctx.promise("payment-completed").resolve(result);
}

// Signal function for human approval
@Handler
public void approveRequest(WorkflowSharedContext ctx, Boolean approved) {
    ctx.promise("manager-approval").resolve(approved);
}
```

```python Python
# Signal function to confirm payment
async def confirm_payment(ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, result: PaymentResult):
    ctx.promise("payment-completed").resolve(result)

# Signal function for human approval
async def approve_request(ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, approved: bool):
    ctx.promise("manager-approval").resolve(approved)
```
</CodeGroup>

## Common coordination patterns

### Human approval workflow

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processExpenseReport = async (ctx: WorkflowContext, report: ExpenseReport) => {
  ctx.set("status", "pending-approval");
  ctx.set("submittedAt", new Date().toISOString());
  
  // Determine approval level needed
  const approvalLevel = report.amount > 1000 ? "director" : "manager";
  ctx.set("approvalLevel", approvalLevel);
  
  // Wait for approval decision
  const approvalResult = await ctx.promise<ApprovalResult>("approval-decision");
  
  if (approvalResult.approved) {
    ctx.set("status", "approved");
    ctx.set("approvedBy", approvalResult.approver);
    ctx.set("approvedAt", new Date().toISOString());
    
    // Process reimbursement
    const reimbursement = await ctx.run("process-reimbursement", () =>
      processReimbursement(report, approvalResult.approver)
    );
    
    ctx.set("status", "reimbursed");
    return { status: "approved", reimbursementId: reimbursement.id };
  } else {
    ctx.set("status", "rejected");
    ctx.set("rejectedBy", approvalResult.approver);
    ctx.set("rejectionReason", approvalResult.reason);
    
    return { status: "rejected", reason: approvalResult.reason };
  }
};

// Signal function for approval
export const submitApproval = async (ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, decision: ApprovalResult) => {
  ctx.promise("approval-decision").resolve(decision);
};
```
</CodeGroup>

### External system integration

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processOrderWithExternalPayment = async (ctx: WorkflowContext, order: Order) => {
  ctx.set("status", "processing");
  
  // Create payment intent with external provider
  const paymentIntent = await ctx.run("create-payment-intent", () =>
    createPaymentIntent(order.total, order.currency)
  );
  
  ctx.set("paymentIntentId", paymentIntent.id);
  ctx.set("status", "awaiting-payment");
  
  try {
    // Wait for webhook from payment provider (with 30 minute timeout)
    const paymentResult = await Promise.race([
      ctx.promise<PaymentWebhook>("payment-webhook"),
      ctx.sleep(30 * 60 * 1000).then(() => {
        throw new Error("Payment timeout");
      })
    ]);
    
    if (paymentResult.status === "succeeded") {
      ctx.set("status", "paid");
      ctx.set("paymentId", paymentResult.id);
      
      // Continue with order fulfillment
      const fulfillment = await ctx.run("fulfill-order", () => fulfillOrder(order));
      
      ctx.set("status", "fulfilled");
      return { status: "completed", fulfillmentId: fulfillment.id };
    } else {
      ctx.set("status", "payment-failed");
      return { status: "failed", reason: "Payment failed" };
    }
    
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.message === "Payment timeout") {
      ctx.set("status", "payment-timeout");
      
      // Cancel the payment intent
      await ctx.run("cancel-payment", () => cancelPaymentIntent(paymentIntent.id));
      
      return { status: "failed", reason: "Payment timeout" };
    }
    throw error;
  }
};

// Signal function for payment webhook
export const handlePaymentWebhook = async (ctx: WorkflowSharedContext, webhook: PaymentWebhook) => {
  ctx.promise("payment-webhook").resolve(webhook);
};
```
</CodeGroup>

### Multi-stage coordination

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processApplicationWorkflow = async (ctx: WorkflowContext, application: JobApplication) => {
  ctx.set("status", "submitted");
  ctx.set("stage", "initial-review");
  
  // Stage 1: Initial screening
  const screening = await ctx.promise<ScreeningResult>("screening-complete");
  if (!screening.passed) {
    ctx.set("status", "rejected");
    ctx.set("stage", "screening");
    return { status: "rejected", reason: screening.reason };
  }
  
  ctx.set("stage", "technical-interview");
  
  // Stage 2: Technical interview
  const technicalResult = await ctx.promise<InterviewResult>("technical-interview-complete");
  if (technicalResult.score < 7) {
    ctx.set("status", "rejected");
    ctx.set("stage", "technical-interview");
    return { status: "rejected", reason: "Technical interview score too low" };
  }
  
  ctx.set("stage", "final-interview");
  
  // Stage 3: Final interview
  const finalResult = await ctx.promise<InterviewResult>("final-interview-complete");
  if (!finalResult.passed) {
    ctx.set("status", "rejected");
    ctx.set("stage", "final-interview");
    return { status: "rejected", reason: finalResult.feedback };
  }
  
  ctx.set("stage", "offer");
  ctx.set("status", "approved");
  
  // Generate offer
  const offer = await ctx.run("generate-offer", () => generateJobOffer(application, technicalResult, finalResult));
  ctx.set("offerId", offer.id);
  
  // Wait for offer response (30 days timeout)
  try {
    const offerResponse = await Promise.race([
      ctx.promise<OfferResponse>("offer-response"),
      ctx.sleep(30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).then(() => ({ accepted: false, reason: "Timeout" }))
    ]);
    
    if (offerResponse.accepted) {
      ctx.set("status", "hired");
      return { status: "hired", startDate: offerResponse.startDate };
    } else {
      ctx.set("status", "offer-declined");
      return { status: "declined", reason: offerResponse.reason };
    }
    
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.set("status", "offer-expired");
    return { status: "expired", reason: "Offer expired" };
  }
};
```
</CodeGroup>

## Best practices

**Use meaningful promise keys**: Include relevant identifiers like order IDs, user IDs, or request IDs in promise keys.

**Combine with timeouts**: Use `Promise.race()` with `ctx.sleep()` to implement timeouts for external events.

**Handle promise rejection gracefully**: External systems might send error data instead of success data.

**Store intermediate state**: Save progress and intermediate results so you can track workflow progression.

**Implement cancellation signals**: Provide signal functions to cancel or modify running workflows.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Good: Descriptive promise key with timeout
const result = await Promise.race([
  ctx.promise<ApprovalResult>(`approval-${requestId}`),
  ctx.sleep(24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).then(() => ({ approved: false, reason: "Timeout" }))
]);

// Good: Store state for tracking
ctx.set("currentStage", "awaiting-approval");
ctx.set("approvalRequestedAt", new Date().toISOString());
```
</CodeGroup>

Workflow coordination helpers enable sophisticated multi-party coordination patterns that would be complex to implement reliably with traditional approaches, while maintaining exactly-once execution guarantees.
